description_long: This object is a large, complex solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple solar panels mounted on a flat, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration, with some panels tilted at an angle and others lying flat. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, with various raised sections, vents, and grid-like textures suggesting integrated electrical or cooling components. The structure includes several blocky, dark-colored supports and possible inverter housings, as well as surface markings and labels. The overall design is industrial and robust, suitable for deployment in urban environments such as rooftops, parking structures, or dedicated solar farms. The visible surfaces are predominantly gray and black, with some lighter blue-gray areas and a few red markings.
description: A large, industrial solar energy installation with multiple tilted and flat solar panels on a robust, platform-like base featuring vents, supports, and grid textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered arrangement of solar panels, raised sections, and grid-like textures on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angles of the solar panels and the elongated, low-profile structure of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the panels, additional raised components, and surface markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angular supports and the overall length of the installation.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are mostly tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, with some flat; orientation appears fixed and not adjustable.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a raised, platform-like base with visible metal supports and brackets, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows in a staggered, clustered configuration; approximately 6-8 panels arranged with varying tilt and spacing.
